cases.name: Sher v. Lafayette Ins. Co.
cases.one_liner: Louisiana Supreme Court decision addressing hurricane-related property damage and policy interpretation issues.

notes: Useful for Louisiana first-party hurricane property disputes involving causation and policy interpretation.

cases.name: Dickerson v. Lexington Ins. Co.
cases.court: United States Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit
cases.one_liner: Fifth Circuit decision frequently cited in Louisiana hurricane-coverage disputes involving wind versus flood causation.

cases.name: In re Katrina Canal Breaches Litig.
cases.court: United States Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit
cases.one_liner: Fifth Circuit opinion frequently referenced for Louisiana flood, levee, and policy causation issues after major storms.

notes: Review how Louisiana and Fifth Circuit authorities treat concurrent wind and flood damage theories.

sources.title: In re Katrina Canal Breaches Litig.
